name: That Dragon, Cancer: I’m Sorry Guys, It’s Not Good

reviews.review_description: This is a vr experience with excellent writing, emotional voice acting, and beautiful graphics. It's also FREE so you have nothing to lose by checking this app out (except for some happiness I suppose because this will give you the feels). Well done!
This is not a fun game

Not all games are shooty shooty bang bang. Some games can make you feel an emotion and give a message. This game is the later.
I dont know if this is the full game because it kept going to the beginning after i finished it

developer_response.body: This is a demo scene built in VR from our full game.  The full game can be purchased on Steam, iOS or Android.

reviews.review_title: A good story is mojo half of a game.
reviews.review_description: Ratings are meant to be honest impressions of a game's overall quality and by labeling this as a game, rather than an experience, it should be held to the standards of a game. As a game, it was only alright.  If you feel empathy for the developer, then donate to a charity or their cause. Do not deceive people into downloading a game to pay some debt to your conscience. It is not heartless or uncaring because you thought the story was touching, but not suited for a game.

Today is the day young Jessica Whelan died of cancer (look her up) so I just had to watch this.
I cannot imagine what it is like to be a parent of a child with terminal cancer. This "game" makes it possible and puts you in their shoes.  Never forget how lucky you are to take your next breath.  If you have kids, cherish them and never take them for granted. Life is so darn  fragile and you never know when the privilege of having it will be taken away. That's what I took from this and I thank the developer for creating this and letting us into his world. And for God's sake let's beat this F disease!!!!!
The most emotional and scary experience i ever had in VR, my Gear is wet now.
An astonishing use of virtual reality that make feel the deep fear of the terrible illnes.

I've been terrified to play this game. I have a 7 and a 3 year old. Knowing what the game is about, I got a bit misty eyed when I looked at the kid and he signed "more." I don't know how to say anything about this game. The developers have allowed us into possibly the worst moment of their lives. It feels wrong to be there, almost voyeuristic, but at the same time, I feel like it's somehow good to be there. I don't know. This messed me up. But it's good. Or rather, very well done.

reviews.review_title: Its ver good but its short
reviews.review_description:
I've Learned about the full game and wanted to try it for some time.

This Version is very well made and emmotional, but I think I didnt have enought time to immerse myself. I would preffer a larger game even if it were more expensive.

But I have liked everything I saw
You can feel a sort of tragedy going on around you...good the sea level raising after some time
I haven't played it yet but I have seen people play it and it makes me cry 5/5
Techically interesting, emotionally investing.
What's the point
By Numinous. Virtual environment for therapy. TRT 8 min.
Play the full game. This scene only scratches the surface.
Unfortunately do to glitchy sound the game is unplayable in my opinion.
Every parents worst nightmare. I held my kids tightly so they squirmed and laughed as they ran off and played. Interesting take on how doctors feel.

reviews.review_title: Short but beautiful piece of art
reviews.review_description: Very low tech graphics but I enjoyed the story and the interaction
